label: First Contract Filed
flavour: Mana-credit transferred. Operation logged. The Iron Company has a first entry in the ledger.

label: Five Operations Completed
flavour: Five contracts in the record. Still operational. The survival rate for new PMCs at this stage is not encouraging. You are ahead of it.

label: Established Operator
flavour: Ten operations. The pattern is holding. The company is not a coincidence anymore.

label: Proven Track Record
flavour: Twenty entries. The clients are reading that number before they brief you now.

label: Operational Continuity
flavour: Thirty contracts. The fort is real. The ledger is real. The Iron Company is beginning to look like something that will still be operational next year.

label: First Personnel Hire
flavour: The company has its first hire. The paperwork is filed. The operation begins to feel real.

label: Five Personnel Signed
desc: Have 5 companions on the active roster.
flavour: Five operators. The Iron Company is a unit now, not a solo act.

label: Roster at Capacity
desc: Fill your roster to maximum capacity.
flavour: Every available personnel slot filled. You are running at full operational strength for your current clearance tier.

label: Capital Reserve
flavour: Ten thousand in reserve. The Iron Company has a real balance sheet.

label: Significant Capital
flavour: Fifty thousand. You are operating at a scale that warrants a registered corporate structure.

label: Regional Profile
flavour: One-fifty. You are a recognised name in operational circles. Clients seek you rather than the reverse.

label: Strategic Reputation
flavour: Eight hundred renown. Strategic analysts at government level have started citing your operational history.

label: Continental Benchmark
flavour: Two thousand. The Iron Company is referenced in military doctrine assessments. The reference is not flattering. That is appropriate.

label: Senior Operator
flavour: Level five. Your field record justifies the billing rate. Clients who were sceptical have stopped being sceptical.

label: Operational Legend
flavour: Level eight. Your professional history is long enough that new contractors reference it in their own intake profiles.

label: Base Infrastructure Active
flavour: Three upgrades commissioned. The fort is functional as an operational base, not merely habitable.

label: Established Operations Base
flavour: Seven upgrades. The fort is now a genuine PMC facility, not a reclaimed ruin with good bones.

label: Maximum Infrastructure
flavour: Every available upgrade commissioned and operational. The fort is running at full capacity on every metric.

label: Practitioner Hired
desc: Have a mana-capable companion on your roster.
flavour: First practitioner on staff. Your company now has active mana-output capability beyond standard operator augmentation.

label: Mana-Capable Formation
flavour: Ten combined. Your company's aggregate mana-output is now a factor on contracts that require it.

label: High-Output Practitioner Unit
flavour: Twenty-five combined. A Conclave assessor would classify this formation as a significant mana-output entity. They would be correct.

label: Warded Perimeter Active
flavour: The perimeter wards are live. Your fort is broadcasting a clear signal to anything operating in the mana-layer.

label: Premium Personnel
flavour: Rare-grade operator on contract. The intake process was complicated. The result justifies it.

label: First Arcane Operation
flavour: First arcane operation completed. The Conclave's compliance desk has probably received an automated alert.

label: Deep-Site Certified
flavour: First deep-site operation filed. The briefing materials for those contracts do not cover everything that is actually down there.

label: Retrieval Record
flavour: Three bounty operations cleared. Your company has a demonstrated track record in the retrieval and termination space.

label: Combat-Rated
flavour: Five direct-action operations. The Iron Company's combat record is now empirically established.

label: Licensed PMC Status
flavour: Tier One. You are now a registered private military contractor with a verified operational base. Nations are taking note.

label: Iron Warband
flavour: Tier Two. You are in the strategic planning documents of at least one government. This is confirmation of relevance.

label: Iron Regiment
flavour: Tier Three. Suppression files have been opened by multiple Accord members. You are operating above the threshold where they can ignore you.

label: Iron Legion
flavour: Tier Four. A national security council mentioned your name in a classified session. They were not discussing neutrality.

label: Iron Nation
flavour: Tier Five. The Iron Company is no longer classifiable as a PMC under any Accord framework. The category above PMC doesn't have an official name yet. You are defining it.

label: Sovereign Force
flavour: You have reached the top of the classification structure. There is no tier above this because no one expected anyone to reach it. You have arrived at a place that did not officially exist.

label: First Suppression Weathered
flavour: A government launched an official suppression operation against the Iron Company. It did not succeed.

label: Suppression-Hardened
flavour: Five suppression operations survived and closed. The Iron Company has been the target of state-level suppression half a decade running and is still operational. That fact is itself intelligence.

label: Threat Classification — Hostile
desc: Have any nation reach hostile relation.
flavour: A national government has formally reclassified the Iron Company as a hostile non-state actor. This is the clearest possible confirmation of strategic relevance.

label: Threat Neutralised
desc: Reduce a hostile nation's threat to zero.
flavour: A nation that committed to your suppression has been walked back to neutrality. The operational lesson has been delivered and received.

label: First Territorial Operation
flavour: First territorial operation completed. The Iron Company now has a documented claim on something that wasn't on any map before.

label: Territorial Presence
flavour: Three territorial operations. The Iron Company's footprint has expanded past the fort perimeter in a formally documented sense.

label: First Intelligence Operation
flavour: First information operation cleared. Your company is now operating in the tier where information is the primary asset.

label: Operational Intelligence Capability
flavour: Five intelligence operations. The Iron Company has a demonstrated information network and the operational history to prove it.

label: Political Operator
flavour: Three political operations completed. Governments now factor the Iron Company into their diplomatic calculus.

label: Multidomain Operator
desc: Complete contracts in territorial, political, and intelligence categories.
flavour: Territorial, political, and intelligence operations all cleared. The Iron Company is operating across every domain that defines a strategic power. The categorisation gap is closing.

label: The General's File
desc: Complete the Defect the General contract.
flavour: The general has been turned. Their operational knowledge is now part of the Iron Company's intelligence picture. Veldrath is reconsidering several standing assumptions.

label: Consistent Record
flavour: Forty operations cleared. The Iron Company is not an anomaly. It is a sustained operational reality.

label: Enduring Operator
flavour: Sixty contracts. The Iron Company has been running long enough to be a reference point for other organisations.
